summary: 9 items surfaced from 22 qualifying stories; the strongest signals clustered around open models, agent safety, one music-learning site, and a COBOL oddity.

items.name: MiMo Code
items.source_story_title: MiMo Code is now released and open-source
items.summary: Xiaomi's open-source coding harness for LLM workflows.
items.why_included: The thread framed open coding harnesses as the right default for agent tooling.
items.evidence: Commenters praised the open release and argued LLM tools should be commodities, not closed platforms.

items.name: OLMo
items.summary: AI2's open language-model stack for reproducible training runs.
items.why_included: Commenters used it as the clearest benchmark for a truly open model pipeline.
items.evidence: The thread said OLMo releases full datasets and is closer to a fully open pipeline than Nemotron.

items.name: Nemotron
items.summary: NVIDIA's open model family with weights, recipes, and data.
items.why_included: The thread treated Nemotron as a strong open-model reference point.
items.evidence: Commenters said Nemotron exposes much of the training stack and has enough openness to serve as a good starting point.

items.name: OpenThoughts
items.summary: Open reasoning-dataset project for training small reasoning models.
items.why_included: HN commenters called it a practical open-data path for reasoning models.
items.evidence: The thread pointed to its dataset, model releases, and curation paper as useful open infrastructure.

items.name: K2 Think V2
items.summary: A fully sovereign open-source reasoning system from MBZUAI.
items.why_included: It surfaced as another end-to-end open reasoning stack with full transparency.
items.evidence: The project page and comments describe it as open from pre-training data through post-training and evaluation.

items.name: Claw Patrol
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Claw Patrol, a security firewall for agents
items.summary: A network-layer firewall for AI agents.
items.why_included: It solves the concrete problem of safely letting agents touch production systems.
items.evidence: Deno described it as a WireGuard/Tailscale firewall that parses HTTP, Postgres, and SSH and gates destructive actions.

items.name: DeltaDB
items.summary: Zed's fine-grained history layer for changes between commits.
items.why_included: The thread turned it into a serious debate about provenance, review, and auditability.
items.evidence: Commenters quoted the idea that DeltaDB records every operation in between commits and argued over whether that history is useful or invasive.

items.name: Ear Training Practice Exercises
items.summary: A free browser-based ear-training site for musicians.
items.why_included: Commenters praised the ad-free simplicity and the adjustable practice mode.
items.evidence: One user called it simple and great; another clarified the lock icon freezes the difficulty level.

items.name: FPS.cob
items.source_story_title: FPS.cob: A first person shooter in COBOL
items.summary: A COBOL first-person shooter built with raycasting and PPM output.
items.why_included: It was a fun retro-tech project that commenters found surprisingly readable.
items.evidence: The thread described the game pipeline and several commenters debated the AI-assisted authorship while still admiring the implementation.
